content_preview.title: Carbon Capture Enters Its Commercial Era as First Megascale DAC and CCS Plants Near Operations
content_preview.summary: With the Stratos direct air capture facility in Texas targeting Q2 startup, California's first CCS project nearing injection, and Norway's Northern Lights expanding, the carbon capture industry is transitioning from pilot projects to commercial-scale operations in 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## The Scale-Up Moment

For years, carbon capture technology has been discussed in the conditional tense — a promising tool that could help decarbonize heavy industry and remove legacy emissions from the atmosphere, if only it could be built at scale and at a price the market would bear. In 2026, the conditional is giving way to the present tense. A cluster of commercial-scale projects across three continents is approaching operations simultaneously, marking what the [International Energy Agency...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent analysis covering the three major CCS/DAC projects (Stratos, CTV I, Northern Lights) with clear technical differentiation and honest assessment of economics.
editor_notes.source_verification: 5 of 7 sources manually verified. OGJ confirmed Stratos Q2 2026 online target, 500,000 tonnes/year capacity, Phase 2 commissioning in Q2. GlobeNewswire confirmed CTV I construction complete at Elk Hills, spring 2026 injection target, EPA Class VI permits, CRC 51%/Brookfield 49% JV. Equinor confirmed Northern Lights Phase 2: NOK 7.5B investment, 5M tonnes/year capacity, 2028 completion. Heirloom BusinessWire confirmed $150M Series B Dec 2024. IEA source returned 403 but is a standard international agency source. WEF source not separately fetched but is a standard institutional source.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified: Stratos 500,000 tonnes capacity, Q2 2026 timeline, Carbon Engineering technology. CTV I: construction complete, spring 2026 injection, Kern County. Northern Lights Phase 2: NOK 7.5B (~$700M), 5M tonnes/year, 2028. Heirloom: $150M Series B, Shreveport LA. 45Q credit rates ($85/ton CCS, $180/ton DAC) match IRA provisions.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Appropriately balanced. Does not oversell carbon capture, explicitly noting the gap between current scale (3-4M tonnes) and global emissions (37B tonnes).
editor_notes.originality: Strong comparative framework covering both DAC and point-source CCS, with useful economic analysis of 45Q credits and cost curves.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Comprehensive and well-verified analysis of carbon capture commercialization. Approved.
